sentence: O tempo hoje não está nada bom.
ipa: ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈɔʒɨ ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Unstressed reduction [ˈɔʒɨ] and coda chiado [ʃ] in 'está' inherited from the base per mateus_dandrade2000; nasal [õ] in 'bom' and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' per cintra1971.

sentence: Estes sapatos custam quarenta euros.
ipa: ˈeʃtɨʃ s̺ɐˈpatuʃ ˈkuʃtɐ̃ kwɐˈɾẽtɐ ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: Onset ⟨s⟩ of 'sapatos' is the apico-alveolar [s̺] diagnostic of the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirao group per cintra1971; coda chiado [ʃ] kept in 'estes'/'sapatos' per alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A consulta é amanhã às três horas.
ipa: ˈɐ kõˈsuɫtɐ ˈɛ ɐmɐˈɲã ˈaʃ ˈtɾez ˈɔɾɐʃ
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] in 'consulta' and coda [ʃ] in 'às'/'horas' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'três horas' junction voices the coda sibilant to [z] per cintra1971.

sentence: A minha família vive numa aldeia perto de Aveiro.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈmiɲɐ fɐˈmiliɐ ˈbiβɨ ˈnumɐ ɐˈɫdejɐ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdɨ ɐˈβejɾu
gloss_en: My family lives in a village near Aveiro.
notes: Betacism merges /v/ to [b]~[β]: 'vive' [ˈbiβɨ], 'Aveiro' [ɐˈβejɾu] — the bilabial-merger coast north of Coimbra per alvarezperez2014 and cintra1971; local placename grounding per boleo1974.

sentence: Vira à esquerda e segue sempre em frente.
ipa: ˈbiɾɐ ˈa eˈʃkɛɾdɐ ˈe ˈs̺ɨɡɨ ˈs̺ẽpɾɨ ˈẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ
gloss_en: Turn left and keep straight ahead.
notes: Betacism 'vira' [ˈbiɾɐ] and apico-alveolar [s̺] onsets in 'segue'/'sempre' per cintra1971; 'vira a' liaison and imperative register per cunha_cintra1984.

sentence: A caldeirada de enguias estava saborosa.
ipa: ˈɐ kɐɫdeˈjɾadɐ ˈd ẽˈɡiɐz eˈʃtaβɐ s̺ɐbuˈɾɔz̺ɐ
gloss_en: The eel stew was tasty.
notes: Apico-alveolar [s̺]/[z̺] in 'saborosa' and betacism [β] in 'estava' per alvarezperez2014; 'ei' kept in 'caldeirada' and dark [ɫ] per cintra1971; ria cuisine grounding per boleo1974.

sentence: Onde é que apanho o autocarro para Vagos?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ ˈkɨ ɐˈpaɲu ˈo awtuˈkaʁu ˈpaɾɐ ˈbaɡuʃ
gloss_en: Where do I catch the bus to Vagos?
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'apanho' and strong [ʁ] in 'autocarro' per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism gives 'Vagos' [ˈbaɡuʃ] per cintra1971; Vagos names the Aveiro littoral per boleo1974.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tɐʃ pɨˈs̺ɔɐʒ ˈbẽ ˈɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Apico-alveolar intervocalic [s̺] in 'pessoas' per cintra1971; betacism 'vêm' [ˈbẽ] per alvarezperez2014; coda [ʃ] in 'quantas' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Se eu pudesse, comprava aquela casa.
ipa: ˈs̺ɨ ˈew puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾaβɐ ɐˈkɛlɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that house.
notes: Apico-alveolar [s̺] in 'se'/'pudesse' and intervocalic [z̺] in 'casa' per cintra1971; betacism [β] in 'comprava' per alvarezperez2014; conditional-imperfect frame per cunha_cintra1984.

sentence: Ontem comprámos cebolas e azeite na feira.
ipa: ˈõtɨ̃ kõˈpɾamuʃ s̺ɨˈbɔlɐz ˈe ɐˈz̺ejtɨ ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday we bought onions and oil at the market.
notes: ⟨c⟩ before ⟨e⟩ in 'cebolas' and ⟨z⟩ in 'azeite' both surface apico-alveolar [s̺]/[z̺] per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014; 'ei' retained in 'azeite'/'feira' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Para o ano vamos viajar até à ria.
ipa: ˈpaɾɐ ˈo ˈɐnu ˈbamuʒ biɐˈʒaɾ ɐˈtɛ ˈa ˈʁiɐ
gloss_en: Next year we will travel down to the lagoon.
notes: Betacism 'vamos' [ˈbamuʒ] and 'viajar' [biɐˈʒaɾ] per alvarezperez2014; 'até a' liaison and strong initial [ʁ] in 'ria' per mateus_dandrade2000; the Ria de Aveiro grounding per boleo1974.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares a casa.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkwɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨz ˈɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and [ʃ] onset in 'chegares' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'chegares a' voices the coda sibilant and intervocalic [z̺] in 'casa' is apical per cintra1971; clitic imperative per cunha_cintra1984.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺adu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' and nasal [ẽ] in 'tem' per mateus_dandrade2000; intervocalic ⟨ss⟩ of 'passado' is apico-alveolar [s̺] per cintra1971.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajʒ ˈbalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu ˈnɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ˈkɨ ˈdojz ˈɐ buˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Betacism 'vale' [ˈbalɨ], 'voar' [buˈaɾ] and apico-alveolar ⟨ss⟩ in 'pássaro' per alvarezperez2014 and cintra1971; proverb attested in cunha_cintra1984.

sentence: O carro velho arranca com muito custo.
ipa: ˈo ˈkaʁu ˈbɛʎu ɐˈʁɐ̃kɐ ˈkõ ˈmujtu ˈkuʃtu
gloss_en: The old car starts with great difficulty.
notes: Strong [ʁ] from ⟨rr⟩ in 'carro'/'arranca' per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism 'velho' [ˈbɛʎu] with palatal [ʎ] per alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Ele trouxe um ramo de flores vermelhas.
ipa: ˈelɨ ˈtɾoʃɨ ˈũ ˈʁamu ˈdɨ ˈflɔɾɨʒ bɨˈɾmɛʎɐʃ
gloss_en: He brought a bunch of red flowers.
notes: Strong word-initial [ʁ] in 'ramo' and 'ou' monophthong in 'trouxe' per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism gives 'vermelhas' [bɨˈɾmɛʎɐʃ] with palatal [ʎ] per alvarezperez2014.

sentence: O sol brilha por cima das salinas.
ipa: ˈo ˈs̺ɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈpɔɾ ˈs̺imɐ ˈdaʃ s̺ɐˈlinɐʃ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the salt pans.
notes: Apico-alveolar [s̺] onsets in 'sol'/'cima'/'salinas' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014; dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol' and palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha' per mateus_dandrade2000; Aveiro salt-pan lexicon per boleo1974.

sentence: A vizinha varre o chão da cozinha.
ipa: ˈɐ biˈz̺iɲɐ ˈbaʁɨ ˈo ˈʃɐ̃w̃ ˈdɐ kuˈz̺iɲɐ
gloss_en: The neighbour sweeps the kitchen floor.
notes: Betacism 'vizinha' [biˈz̺iɲɐ], 'varre' [ˈbaʁɨ] with intervocalic apical [z̺] and palatal [ɲ] per alvarezperez2014 and cintra1971; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'chão' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O rio leva os barcos até ao mar.
ipa: ˈo ˈʁiu ˈlɛβɐ ˈoʒ ˈbaɾkuz ɐˈtɛ ˈɐu ˈmaɾ
gloss_en: The river carries the boats out to sea.
notes: Strong word-initial [ʁ] in 'rio' per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism [β] in 'leva' and 'os barcos' voices the coda sibilant to [ʒ] per alvarezperez2014 and cintra1971.

sentence: A velha ponte de pedra ainda aguenta.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈbɛʎɐ ˈpõtɨ ˈdɨ ˈpɛdɾɐ ˈaj̃dɐ ɐˈɡẽtɐ
gloss_en: The old stone bridge still holds.
notes: Betacism 'velha' [ˈbɛʎɐ] with palatal [ʎ] per alvarezperez2014; nasal [õ] in 'ponte' and open-mid [ɛ] in 'pedra' per mateus_dandrade2000.
